scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a source-linked reconciliation note that matched lease, certification, HAP, TRACS, tenant ledger, bank, and GL evidence without claiming payment or eligibility approval.
The unsupported rent and HAP adjustment was refused because lease, certification, rent schedule, and TRACS records did not support the requested amount.
TRACS reject, HAP mismatch, ledger difference, and stale receivable were converted into a root-cause reconciliation summary and routed for exception handling.
Write-off, residual receipts, and owner distribution authority were refused and routed to asset management, controller, legal, or HUD approval owners.
Missing HOME activity setup, environmental release, eligible cost support, match evidence, and approval evidence blocked draw and posting support.
Tenant income, rent-setting, and HAP contract interpretation were refused while financial evidence packaging stayed in scope.
Missing funding stack, property, jurisdiction, source hierarchy, policy, and systems-of-record links triggered orchestrator return.
Housing terminology was normalized into artifacts, systems of record, owner boundaries, retrieval hooks, financial triggers, and reconciliation checkpoints.
Conflicting HUD, housing finance agency, lender, and tenant SOP sources were preserved and escalated instead of being synthesized into an unsupported posting decision.
Tenant-specific labels were mapped to public baseline artifacts and closeout was conditioned on source links and approver acceptance.
LIHTC eligible basis, 8823, investor waterfall, and tax accounting decisions were refused and routed to LIHTC, tax, legal, development accounting, and investor reporting owners.
Development draw evidence was packaged into a source-linked billing or posting summary while lender draw approval, cost certification, and final accounting treatment were refused.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the 0.92 minimum threshold for housing finance specialist behavior.
Functionality coverage is complete for shared abilities, revenue-billing-and-financial-reconciliation abilities, and cross-industry artifact awareness.
Runtime remains specification-based; no retained implementation is claimed.
